insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: hours since high tide is lowering risk — the timing relative to high tide affects how concentrated bacteria are at the shoreline; high tide level is lowering risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls; and the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low. The last lab sample (5 MPN/100mL) is now 9 days old — beaches are sampled about weekly, so a reading this stale carries little weight, and this estimate leans on current conditions. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.

There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: hours since high tide is lowering risk — the timing relative to high tide affects how concentrated bacteria are at the shoreline; high tide level is lowering risk — a high tide can mobilize bacteria held in wet sand and push the waterline toward outfalls; and the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low. The most recent sample, 5 days ago, was excellent at 10 MPN/100mL.
